title: terminal broken inside of podman with usernamespace in some images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

The terminal is pretty broken on some images when running with `--userns=keep-id`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -ti --userns=keep-id docker.io/greyrook/cde-dev-py37:cbfa6f05455445fbdc86acc3c7d7945c42c61829 bash`

2. press `aa[backspace]`


**Describe the results you received:**

`[I have no name!@7c735c260625 /]$ a`
`[I have no name!@7c735c260625 /]$ aa`
`[I have no name!@7c735c260625 /]$ aa  ` <- space entered after `aa`


**Describe the results you expected:**

`[I have no name!@7c735c260625 /]$ a`
backspace working to delete stuff


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This issue was not present in some earlier version of podman.

comments.body:
I am not able to reproduce using both crun and runc from upstream.

Do you see the same issue if you install crun and try?

```
podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run -ti --userns=keep-id docker.io/greyrook/cde-dev-py37:cbfa6f05455445fbdc86acc3c7d7945c42c61829 bash
```
Could you also try to disable temporarily selinux?  It could also be causing issues with the terminal

Hi @giuseppe ,

> Do you see the same issue if you install crun and try?
> 
> ```
> podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run -ti --userns=keep-id docker.io/greyrook/cde-dev-py37:cbfa6f05455445fbdc86acc3c7d7945c42c61829 bash
> ```

Produces the exact same result.

![term](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/432263/87413004-010e4700-c5ca-11ea-9e7f-3d805afd00f7.gif)


> Could you also try to disable temporarily selinux? It could also be causing issues with the terminal
Interesting, didn't know that :)  But didn't make a difference.


---
I tried using a different user on the same machine - and didn't have an issue. Maybe it's one of podmans configs - but I always forget where to find confuse which config it actually uses :sweat: ...

With `--log-level debug`:

DEBU[0000] Ignoring libpod.conf EventsLogger setting "/home/f.ludwig/.config/containers/containers.conf". Use "journald" if you want to change this setting and remove libpod.conf files. 

But `/home/f.ludwig/.config/containers/` does not even exist.

```console
 [I have no name!@7c735c260625 /]$ aa <- space entered after aa
```
When I see "backspace sends space", my first thought is termcap. What is `$TERM` set to in your invoking shell? Does your container have a corresponding termcap entry for that term? Can you try running the container with `TERM=xterm` (or something plain and simple)?

EDIT: I just watched your video and saw "unknown terminal type xterm". Yep, that's your problem. Maybe try `TERM=vt100`? Anyway, this is not a podman problem.

@edsantiago 
```
TERM=vt100 podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run -ti --userns=keep-id docker.io/greyrook/cde-dev-py37:cbfa6f05455445fbdc86acc3c7d7945c42c61829 bash
```
produces the same result.

Setting it inside the container, does also not change it. Interestingly `reset` still errors out:
```
$ reset
reset: unknown terminal type vt100
```
My point is, you need to use a termcap entry that is supported by your container. Or, if the container is under your control, install (or don't delete) termcap entries.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe this is not a bug in Podman, reopen if you disagree.
